title: zosterophyllanthos sigynes
side_effects: Zosterophyllanthos sigynes can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, causing irritation to the skin, and triggering anaphylaxis in severe cases. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In rare instances, it may also cause cardiovascular problems, such as hypertension and arrhythmia, especially in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant may result in contact dermatitis and skin ulcers.
